intro: Thelypteris novae-brittaniae, also known as the New Britain fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to New Britain in Papua New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris novae-brittaniae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
